content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
June 6, 1997
Pat Dowden
9400 Forest Vista Way
Elk Grove, California 95758
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-295
Dear Mr. Dowden:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
For purposes of Section 85305 and the date when you may begin fundraising for your
June 1998 election, does Sacramento County have more than one million residents?
CONCLUSION
You have been informed that the population of Sacramento County has been over
1,000,000 residents since 1990 and that it currently stands at a 1996 estimate of 1,123,400.
Therefore, you may begin fundraising for your June 1998 election on June 2, 1997.
FACTS
You intend to be a candidate for Sheriff of Sacramento County on the June 2, 1998,
ballot. You intend to kick-off your campaign on June 21, 1997, and you will begin to receive
contributions at that time. On May 12, 1997, you received information from Erica Fogle,
Community Services Planning Council, that the population for Sacramento County has exceeded
one million since 1990, and currently stands at a 1996 estimate of 1,123,400.
Government Code sections 81000- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-295
Page 2
ANALYSIS
Section 85305(b) provides that in districts of 1 million residents or more and for
statewide elective office, candidates may not accept contributions more than twelve months
before any primary or, in the event there is no primary, any general election. Candidates running
for office in districts with 1 million residents may begin fundraising for their June 1998 elections
on June 2, 1997.
The Commission has not adopted any regulations regarding how to determine whether a
district has 1 million residents. We have advised that a local jurisdiction may utilize the most
recently available census and other reliable date to determine the amount ofresidents in a district.
In short, any reasonable method of detennining this number is acceptable. You have been
informed by a reliable source that the population of Sacramento County is over 1 million
residents. Therefore, you may begin fundraising on June 2, 1997, for your campaign for sheriff
of Sacramento County.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Marte Castafios
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:MC:ak

CONCLUSION: You have been informed that the population of Sacramento County has been over

Therefore, you may begin fundraising for your June 1998 election on June 2, 1997.
